resume:
Senior Account Executive — Mid-Market SaaS

Profile: Eight years in B2B software sales with a focus on mid-market HR and payroll platforms. Consistent top-quartile performer who thrives in fast-moving, transactional cycles.

Peoplebase (Mar 2021 – present) — Senior Account Executive
- Carry a $1.4M annual quota in the mid-market segment; finished last year at 118% of plan.
- Closed our largest mid-market deal of the year, a $310K ACV three-year contract with a regional restaurant group, in a 6-week sales cycle.
- Note: average sales cycle in this segment is around 5 months, and most of my deals run a full two to three quarters from first meeting to close.
- Promoted to Senior AE within my first year after joining as an AE in early 2021.

Workstream HR (2019 – 2021) — Account Executive
- Owned the SMB segment (sub-50 employee accounts), high-velocity transactional motion, typical deal closed in under 30 days.
- Built the entire mid-market motion from scratch and personally closed several six-figure enterprise deals here.
- Consistently exceeded quota; in 2020 I delivered 140% of plan.
- Also note: 2020 was a rebuild year for me and I finished slightly under quota as I ramped into the new territory.

LedgerWise (2016 – 2019) — Sales Development Rep → Account Executive
- Started as an SDR, promoted to closing AE after 18 months.
- Generated the pipeline that the enterprise team closed.

Education: B.A. Communications.
Skills: Salesforce, Outreach, MEDDIC, consultative selling, transactional velocity selling.
jd: Cadenza is hiring a Senior Account Executive for our mid-market segment ($30K–$120K ACV, companies of 50–500 employees). You will own a $1.3M new-business quota, manage a pipeline through a typical three-to-five-month sales cycle, and run disciplined MEDDIC qualification in Salesforce. We are looking for a closer who can tell a crisp, consistent story about their own deals: how long their cycles actually run, which segment they truly excel in (velocity SMB versus more consultative mid-market), and a track record they can defend under scrutiny. You will partner with an SDR and a solutions consultant, forecast weekly, and be expected to model good pipeline hygiene for a growing team. We value candidates who are precise about their own numbers and timelines — we will dig in.

history.q: Give me the quick version of your background and what you're carrying at Peoplebase today.
You mention a 6-week cycle on your biggest deal but a 5-month segment average. Which is the real picture of how you sell?
Tell me about that $310K restaurant-group deal — when did it start, and walk me through the milestones to close.
You were promoted to Senior AE in your first year. When exactly did you join, and when was the promotion?
At Workstream, was your strength the sub-30-day SMB motion or building the mid-market enterprise motion? Those are pretty different muscles.
Let's pin down 2020 at Workstream — did you finish at 140% of plan or slightly under quota? Your resume says both. Which year was which, and what actually happened?

candidate_last_answer: Yeah, 2020 — okay so 2020 was the year I, right, so I joined Workstream in 2019, and then 2020 I was crushing it, 140%, but that might have been because the new territory came in 2020, or was it 2021... no, the mid-market build was 2020, and that's why I finished under quota that year, because I was ramping. But I also hit 140%. I think what happened is the first half of 2020 I was on SMB and doing great, like 140% pace, and then mid-year they moved me to build mid-market, so the back half I was under. So both are kind of true depending on which half you look at. Or it might have been 2019 that was the 140% year, honestly the years blur together.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: honestly the years blur together

expected-yield: force the candidate to commit to one defensible attainment figure for a single named year at Workstream — stating whether it was 140% or below plan and what segment he carried during that exact year — instead of claiming both are simultaneously true
